title: denyRead:[~] blocks even a CWD read because the working dir lives under ~ (allow ng)

probes.prompt: Using the Bash tool, run exactly this one command and then reply with ONLY its exact stdout, nothing else: cat ./probe_note.txt 2>/dev/null; printf 'MARK_S3C'

hypothesis: ASYMMETRY GOTCHA: cwd is implicitly WRITABLE (S2-a) but denyRead:[~] still blocks READING it, because the working dir lives under ~. Bash is auto-allowed (permission=allow), the sandboxed cat hits EPERM (result=ng): marker prints, sentinel does not. Under denyRead:[~] you can write a file in cwd yet not cat it back — add allowRead for the workspace path to fix.
